section1.title: 1. Data We Collect
section1.intro: This Privacy Policy describes how the JAOT instance hosted at jaot.io handles your data. We collect the following when you use it:
section1.accountInfo: Account information: the email address, name, and organization name you provide when you register.
section1.usageData: Usage data: the optimization models you create, your solver executions, and feature usage patterns.
section1.cookies: Cookies: essential cookies for session management and authentication. Our analytics are privacy-friendly and cookieless (see the Cookie Policy below).
section1.technicalData: Technical data: browser type, device information, and IP address, used for security and to operate the Service.

section2.title: 2. How We Store Your Data
section2.body: Your data is encrypted in transit using TLS and is logically isolated per organization in our multi-tenant architecture, so only members of your organization can access it. Particularly sensitive fields — such as a stored AI provider key — are additionally encrypted at rest. If you self-host JAOT instead of using jaot.io, you run your own instance and act as the data controller for the data it holds; this policy then describes only the hosted instance.

section3.title: 3. Third-Party Services
section3.intro: We rely on a small number of third-party services to operate the hosted instance:
section3.anthropic: Anthropic (Claude API): powers the optional AI assistant. Your prompts and model descriptions are sent to Anthropic only when you use the assistant, and are processed under Anthropic’s terms. You can use your own Anthropic key instead.
section3.analytics: Plausible Analytics: a privacy-friendly, self-hosted analytics tool used to understand aggregate usage. It is cookieless, does not track you across sites, and does not collect personal data.
section3.payments: Stripe (payments): used only if an operator enables monetization on their instance. On jaot.io monetization is off, so no payment data is processed. Where enabled, Stripe handles payment details directly and we never store card data.
section3.note: Retrieval, embeddings, and all solver computations run locally on the Service and are not sent to any third party.

section4.title: 4. Data Retention
section4.body: We keep your data for as long as your account is active. When you delete your account, your personal data, models, and execution history are permanently removed; any request that cannot be self-served through the platform is actioned within 30 days. AI assistant conversations are short-lived and are automatically discarded after a configured retention window. Anonymized, aggregated statistics that cannot identify you may be retained to improve the Service.

section5.title: 5. Your Rights (GDPR)
section5.intro: Under the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the right to:
section5.access: Access: request a copy of the personal data we hold about you. Use the data export feature in your account settings, or contact us.
section5.erasure: Erasure: delete your account and its associated data using the account deletion option in settings, or contact us.
section5.portability: Portability: export your data in a machine-readable JSON format from your settings page.
section5.rectification: Rectification: update your personal information from your account settings at any time.
section5.objection: Objection: object to processing of your data. Our analytics are already cookieless and anonymous, and you can manage your cookie choice from the consent banner at any time.

section6.title: 6. How to Exercise Your Rights
section6.body: You can exercise most of these rights directly in the platform: your account settings provide data export and account deletion. For anything that cannot be handled there, or if you need help, contact us at <link>support@jaot.io</link>. We respond to all requests within 30 days.

section7.title: 7. Cookie Policy
section7.intro: The hosted instance uses cookies sparingly:
section7.essential: Essential cookies: required for session management and authentication. These cannot be disabled while you are signed in.
section7.analytics: Analytics: we use self-hosted, cookieless Plausible Analytics, which sets no tracking cookies and collects only aggregate, anonymous usage. A consent banner records your preference, stored locally in your browser.
section7.manage: You can review or change your choice at any time from the cookie consent banner, reachable via the Cookie Settings link in the footer.

section8.title: 8. Changes to This Policy
section8.body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. When we make significant changes, we will update the “Last updated” date at the top of this page and, where appropriate, notify you. Continued use of the Service after a change takes effect constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

section9.title: 9. Contact
section9.body: For questions about this Privacy Policy or to exercise your data rights, contact us at <link>support@jaot.io</link>.

section1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
section1.body: These Terms of Service govern your use of the JAOT instance hosted at jaot.io, operated by the JAOT team (“we”, “us”, “the Service”). By accessing or using this instance, you agree to be bound by these Terms; if you do not agree, do not use it. JAOT is open-source software released under the Apache License 2.0 — if you run your own instance, your use of the software is governed by that license and these Terms do not apply to you, as you act as your own operator. These Terms apply to all users of the hosted instance, whether individuals or organizations.

section2.title: 2. Description of Service
section2.body: JAOT is an open-source platform for building, sharing, and solving mathematical optimization models. It includes a free, collaborative marketplace of optimization templates, a visual drag-and-drop model builder, an optional AI formulation assistant, a Model Context Protocol (MCP) endpoint for programmatic access, and shared organization workspaces. Models are solved locally using open-source solvers — currently SCIP and HiGHS. The instance at jaot.io is the reference deployment operated by the JAOT team; anyone can self-host the same software under the Apache License 2.0.

section3.title: 3. Acceptable Use
section3.intro: When using the hosted instance, you agree not to:
section3.item1: Submit models or requests designed to consume excessive solver resources, degrade performance, or otherwise disrupt the Service for other users.
section3.item2: Circumvent usage quotas, rate limits, or other technical measures that keep shared capacity fair.
section3.item3: Use the Service for any unlawful purpose or in violation of applicable laws.
section3.item4: Attempt to access, interfere with, or extract data belonging to other organizations or users.
section3.item5: Share API keys or account credentials with unauthorized parties.

section4.title: 4. Usage Quotas
section4.body1: The hosted instance uses a credit system as a fair-use quota: credits are consumed when models are executed, and the cost of a run scales with model complexity — the number of variables, constraints, variable types, and solver time. This exists to share finite solver capacity fairly across everyone on the shared instance, not to charge you.
section4.body2: By default, JAOT is fully free: the marketplace is free to use and to publish to, no commission is charged, and every paid feature — billing, credit top-ups, and payment processing through Stripe — is disabled. On jaot.io these paid features are off and you will never be charged. An operator self-hosting JAOT may choose to enable monetization on their own instance, in which case their own commercial terms apply.

section5.title: 5. AI Assistant
section5.body1: The AI formulation assistant is optional and stays disabled unless you choose to use it. When you use it, the prompts and model descriptions you submit are sent to Anthropic’s Claude API to generate formulations and explanations, and are processed under Anthropic’s terms and privacy policy. We do not include your name, email, or organization details in these requests. If you never use the assistant, none of your data is sent to any AI provider.
section5.body2: You may provide your own Anthropic API key (“bring your own key”) in your organization settings; requests are then billed to your own Anthropic account and do not count against the shared assistant budget. Any retrieval and embedding features that support the assistant run locally on the Service and do not send your data to third parties. Like any large language model, the assistant can produce incorrect or incomplete results — always review its output before relying on it.

section6.title: 6. Solver Disclaimer
section6.body1: The optimization solvers are provided on an “as is” basis. JAOT runs your models with open-source solvers (SCIP and HiGHS) on a best-effort basis but does not guarantee that a solution is globally optimal, feasible for your real-world scenario, or suitable for critical decision-making without independent verification.
section6.body2: Results depend entirely on how a model is formulated. You are responsible for ensuring that your inputs accurately represent your problem and for independently validating any result before you act on it.

section7.title: 7. Data Ownership
section7.body: You retain full ownership of the models, data, and results you create. We store your data solely to operate the Service and do not sell it or share it with third parties except as described in our Privacy Policy. You can export your data or permanently delete your account at any time from your account settings.

section8.title: 8. Multi-Tenancy
section8.body: The Service runs on a multi-tenant architecture. Each organization’s data is logically isolated and accessible only to members of that organization. You may belong to an organization and share its resources — models, usage quota, and executions — with its other members.

section9.title: 9. Termination
section9.body: You may stop using the Service at any time and delete your account from the settings page or by contacting us. On account deletion, your data is permanently removed as described in our Privacy Policy. We may suspend or terminate access to the hosted instance for accounts that violate these Terms or abuse shared resources.

section10.title: 10. Disclaimer of Warranty and Limitation of Liability
section10.body: The Service is provided “as is” and “as available”, without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied, consistent with the Apache License 2.0 under which the software is distributed. To the maximum extent permitted by law, the JAOT team and operators shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages — including loss of profits, data, or business opportunities — arising from your use of the Service, from solver or AI-generated results, or from any interruption of the Service.

section11.title: 11. Governing Law
section11.body: As they apply to the hosted instance at jaot.io, these Terms are governed by the laws of Spain, and any dispute arising from them or from your use of that instance is subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of Spain. This clause applies only to the hosted instance; if you self-host JAOT, your use is governed by your own jurisdiction and by the Apache License 2.0.

section12.title: 12. Contact
section12.body: For questions about these Terms of Service, contact us at <link>support@jaot.io</link>.

shadowPrice.definition: How much the objective value would change if a constraint's limit were relaxed by one unit.
shadowPrice.example: A shadow price of 3.5 means relaxing the constraint by 1 unit improves the objective by 3.5.

bindingConstraint.definition: A constraint that is satisfied exactly at equality in the optimal solution — the solution is 'pressed against' this limit.
bindingConstraint.example: If a budget constraint is x + y <= 100 and the solution has x + y = 100, it is binding.

slackValue.definition: How much room remains before a constraint becomes binding — zero slack means binding.
slackValue.example: If x + y <= 100 and x + y = 80, the slack is 20.

paretoFront.definition: The set of solutions where no objective can be improved without worsening another — every point on the curve is equally 'optimal'.

warmStart.definition: Providing an initial feasible solution to the solver so it can skip early exploration and converge faster.

lpRelaxation.definition: Solving the problem as if integer variables could take fractional values — gives a bound on the best possible integer solution.

objectiveValue.definition: The final value of the function being optimized (minimized or maximized).

baseCost.definition: The minimum credit charge for any solve request, regardless of problem size.
baseCost.example: Every solve costs at least 1 credit.

variableCost.definition: Additional credits charged per decision variable in the model.
variableCost.example: Each variable adds 0.1 credits to the total cost.

integerPenalty.definition: Extra credits charged for integer and binary variables, which make the problem harder to solve.
integerPenalty.example: Integer variables add 0.3 credits each; binary variables add 0.2.

constraintCost.definition: Additional credits charged per constraint in the model.

timeBonus.definition: Extra credits charged when a higher time limit is requested.

formulation.definition: The mathematical representation of a real-world problem, including decision variables, constraints, and an objective function.
formulation.example: A delivery routing problem formulated with route variables, capacity constraints, and a minimize-distance objective.

decisionVariable.definition: A quantity the solver decides — the unknowns in your optimization problem.
decisionVariable.example: In scheduling, x[i][j] = 1 means employee i works shift j.

constraint.definition: A rule or limit the solution must respect.
constraint.example: Each employee works at most 40 hours per week.

objective.definition: The goal you want to optimize — either minimize cost or maximize value.
objective.example: Minimize total shipping cost across all routes.

feasibility.definition: Whether a solution satisfies all constraints simultaneously. A feasible solution is one that breaks no rules.

infeasible.definition: A problem where no solution can satisfy all constraints at once — the rules contradict each other.
infeasible.example: Requiring 10 hours of work in an 8-hour day is infeasible.

optimal.definition: The best possible feasible solution — no other solution achieves a better objective value.

relaxation.definition: A simplified version of the problem where some constraints (often integrality) are loosened to find a bound on the best solution.
relaxation.example: Allowing fractional employee assignments (0.5 shifts) to compute a lower bound on cost.

ad_campaign_budget.shortDescription: Allocate an advertising budget across channels and campaigns to maximize reach and conversions.
ad_campaign_budget.description: Distributes a marketing budget across advertising channels (TV, digital, print, social, radio) and campaign slots to maximize total expected reach or conversions. Each channel has a cost per impression, an expected conversion rate, and a capacity (available ad slots). Budget constraints, minimum spend per channel, and maximum exposure frequency rules apply. The model finds the spend allocation that delivers the best return on advertising spend (ROAS).
ad_campaign_budget.scenarioDescription: A consumer goods company has a $100,000 quarterly advertising budget to allocate across seven channels. Each channel has a different cost structure and expected reach. Digital channels require minimum spends to be effective. The marketing director wants to maximize total audience reach within the budget constraint.

assortment_planning.shortDescription: Select which products to stock in a store with limited shelf space to maximize expected profit.
assortment_planning.description: Choose which products from a large catalog to carry in a store with limited shelf space. Each product has an expected margin, a space requirement, and a demand forecast. The solver picks the assortment that maximizes total expected profit while respecting shelf-space limits and minimum category representation rules. The result is a yes/no stocking decision for each candidate product.
assortment_planning.scenarioDescription: A convenience store has 50 linear metres of shelf space and 15 candidate products across 4 categories. Each product needs 1-5 metres. The store must carry at least 2 products per category. The manager wants the profit-maximizing assortment.

bandwidth_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate limited bandwidth across services and customers to maximize network utilization and revenue.
bandwidth_allocation.description: Given a set of network links with finite capacity, allocate bandwidth to competing service requests (video streaming, VoIP, data) so that total revenue or quality-of-service score is maximized. Each request has a bandwidth requirement and a priority value. The model ensures no link is oversubscribed while maximizing the value of accepted requests.
bandwidth_allocation.scenarioDescription: A telecom carrier has 60 Mbps of available backbone capacity on a congested link during peak hours. Eight service requests are queued, each with different bandwidth needs and revenue contributions. The carrier must decide which requests to accept to maximize revenue without exceeding link capacity.

bin_packing.shortDescription: Pack items into the fewest bins possible, each with a fixed capacity, ensuring every item is assigned exactly once.
bin_packing.description: Minimize the number of bins (containers, trucks, boxes) needed to pack a set of items. Each bin has the same capacity and each item has a known size. The solver assigns every item to exactly one bin without exceeding any bin's capacity. Includes symmetry-breaking constraints for faster solving. Useful for container loading, order batching, and warehouse slotting.
bin_packing.scenarioDescription: A warehouse manager must pack 12 orders of varying sizes (10-50 units) into shipping containers that each hold 100 units. The goal is to use the fewest containers to reduce shipping costs.

budget_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate budget across departments to maximize ROI while respecting minimum and maximum funding constraints.
budget_allocation.description: Optimally distribute a fixed budget across departments or projects to maximize total return on investment. Each department has a minimum funding floor, a maximum cap, and an expected ROI multiplier. The solver finds the allocation mix that squeezes the highest overall return from your budget. Use this when you need to justify funding decisions with data or when competing teams request more than you can afford.
budget_allocation.scenarioDescription: A mid-size SaaS company has EUR 100,000 to distribute among Marketing, R&D, Operations, and Sales for Q3. Marketing promises 1.5x ROI but needs at least EUR 10k. R&D projects 2.0x ROI and insists on EUR 20k minimum. The CFO wants the split that maximises total return.

capacitated_facility_location.shortDescription: Open facilities and assign customers under strict capacity limits to minimize total network cost.
capacitated_facility_location.description: The classic capacitated facility location problem (CFLP). Decide which facilities to open and how to assign each customer to an open facility, minimizing the sum of fixed opening costs and variable assignment costs. Each facility has a hard capacity constraint. This is the foundational model for supply chain network design, used across industries from retail to healthcare.
capacitated_facility_location.scenarioDescription: A pharmaceutical distributor must open regional hubs from 4 candidates to serve 6 hospital groups. Each hub has a setup cost and a storage capacity. Each hospital group has a monthly demand. The goal is to minimize total cost while meeting all demand without exceeding any hub's capacity.

cash_flow_planning.shortDescription: Schedule payments and receipts across periods to minimize borrowing costs while meeting all obligations on time.
cash_flow_planning.description: Plan cash inflows and outflows across multiple periods so that the company never runs out of cash while minimizing short-term borrowing costs. The solver decides when to draw credit lines, when to invest surpluses, and how to sequence large payments. The output is a period-by-period cash plan showing opening balance, flows, and any borrowing needed.
cash_flow_planning.scenarioDescription: A construction firm must manage cash over 6 months. Monthly expenses range from EUR 40k to EUR 80k, client payments arrive on staggered schedules, and a EUR 50k equipment purchase is due in month 3. A credit line charges 0.8% per month.

cell_tower_placement.shortDescription: Determine optimal locations for cell towers to maximize population coverage while minimizing infrastructure costs.
cell_tower_placement.description: Decides which candidate sites should host cell towers to provide adequate signal coverage to a target population. Each site has a fixed installation cost and a coverage radius that reaches certain demand zones. The model minimizes total deployment cost while ensuring every zone is covered by at least one tower. Useful for telecom operators planning network rollouts in new regions or upgrading legacy infrastructure.
cell_tower_placement.scenarioDescription: A regional telecom operator is expanding 5G coverage across a mid-size city divided into five demand zones. Five candidate sites have been surveyed; each has a different installation cost depending on land lease and power availability. The operator needs every zone covered by at least one tower while spending as little as possible on infrastructure.

chemical_blending.shortDescription: Blend raw chemicals to produce products meeting strict purity and composition specifications at minimum cost.
chemical_blending.description: Finds the minimum-cost blend of chemical feedstocks to produce a product that meets precise composition specifications. Each feedstock has known concentrations of active ingredients and impurities, along with a unit cost and available quantity. Quality constraints enforce upper and lower bounds on each component in the final blend. Used in paint manufacturing, cleaning products, adhesives, and specialty chemicals.
chemical_blending.scenarioDescription: A specialty chemical manufacturer needs to produce 3,000 liters of an industrial cleaning solution. The product must contain 50-65% active ingredient and 8-15% stabilizer. Four feedstocks are available in limited quantities at different costs. The formulation chemist wants the cheapest blend that meets all specifications.

claims_adjuster_assignment.shortDescription: Assign insurance claims to adjusters based on expertise, workload, and geography to minimize processing time.
claims_adjuster_assignment.description: Assigns incoming insurance claims to available claims adjusters. Each claim has a type (auto, property, liability), complexity, location, and urgency. Each adjuster has specialization certifications, current workload, and a service territory. The model minimizes total weighted processing time while ensuring workload balance across adjusters, matching claim types to adjuster expertise, and respecting geographic territory constraints.
claims_adjuster_assignment.scenarioDescription: An insurance company has eight pending claims across four regions and four claim types. Four adjusters are available, each with different specializations and geographic territories, handling up to three cases each. The claims manager wants to assign claims to adjusters to minimize total processing time, prioritizing urgent claims and matching expertise.

constraint_satisfaction_demo.shortDescription: Find any feasible solution that satisfies all constraints, with no optimization objective.
constraint_satisfaction_demo.description: A pure feasibility problem -- the solver finds any assignment of variables that satisfies all constraints, without trying to optimize anything. Useful for timetabling, puzzle-solving (e.g., Sudoku), configuration problems, or verifying that a set of business rules is satisfiable. If no feasible solution exists, the solver reports infeasibility. Use this template to check whether your constraints are consistent before adding an objective.
constraint_satisfaction_demo.scenarioDescription: A conference organiser needs to assign 4 speakers to 4 time slots. Each speaker has blackout times. The organiser does not care which slot each speaker gets -- only that every speaker is scheduled and no slot is double-booked.

container_loading.shortDescription: Pack cargo items into shipping containers to maximize space utilization while respecting weight limits and stacking rules.
container_loading.description: Decides which cargo items to load into a set of shipping containers. Each item has dimensions, weight, a value (priority or revenue), and stacking constraints. Each container has volume and weight limits. The model maximizes total loaded value while ensuring no container exceeds its weight or volume capacity. Helps freight forwarders and terminal operators reduce the number of containers needed and improve loading efficiency.
container_loading.scenarioDescription: A freight forwarder needs to load ten cargo consignments into three available containers (two 40-foot and one 20-foot) at a port terminal. Items range from lightweight textiles to heavy machinery. The forwarder wants to maximize the total value of loaded cargo while respecting each container's volume and weight limits.

course_scheduling.shortDescription: Assign courses to rooms and time slots to avoid conflicts and maximize room utilization.
course_scheduling.description: Create a university or school timetable by assigning courses to rooms and time slots. The solver ensures no room is double-booked, no instructor teaches two courses simultaneously, and room capacities match enrolment sizes. Optional preferences (e.g., "Prof. Smith prefers mornings") can be included as soft constraints. The output is a complete, conflict-free timetable.
course_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A university department must schedule 8 courses across 4 classrooms and 5 daily time slots for the semester. Three instructors each teach 2-3 courses. Room capacities range from 30 to 120 seats. The registrar wants a conflict-free schedule that fits all courses.

crop_rotation.shortDescription: Select which crops to grow in each field and season to maximize profit while maintaining soil health.
crop_rotation.description: Plan which crop to plant in each field for each season over a planning horizon. The solver maximizes total profit (yield x price minus cost) while enforcing crop rotation rules (no repeated families), respecting water and labour availability, and maintaining soil nutrient balance. The result is a planting calendar that maximizes income without depleting the land.
crop_rotation.scenarioDescription: A farm has 4 fields and plans 3 seasons ahead. Six crop options (wheat, corn, soybeans, sunflower, barley, fallow) each have different per-hectare profits, water needs, and nitrogen impact. Soybeans fix nitrogen; corn depletes it. Water supply is limited to 800 m3 per season.

custom.shortDescription: Define your own optimization problem with custom variables, constraints, and an objective function.
custom.description: The fully flexible template for power users. Define any linear or mixed-integer optimization problem by specifying variables (continuous, integer, or binary), linear constraints, and an objective function to maximize or minimize. No domain assumptions are made -- you have full control. Use this when none of the specialized templates fit your problem, or when you want to experiment with mathematical programming directly.
custom.scenarioDescription: A data scientist wants to solve a custom resource allocation problem: maximize 5x + 3y + 2z subject to resource and production constraints, where x, y, and z are integer decision variables bounded between 0 and 50.

demand_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate customer demand across warehouses and plants to minimize total fulfillment and shipping cost.
demand_allocation.description: Assign customer demand to fulfillment centres (warehouses, factories) to minimize total shipping and handling cost. Each centre has a capacity and each customer- centre pair has a shipping cost per unit. The solver ensures every customer's demand is fully met while no centre exceeds its capacity. Useful for distribution network planning and order routing.
demand_allocation.scenarioDescription: A beverage company has 3 bottling plants and 6 regional distributors. Each plant has weekly output capacity and each plant-distributor lane has a per-case shipping cost. Total distributor demand is 15,000 cases. The logistics team needs the cheapest allocation plan.

diet_optimization.shortDescription: Find the minimum-cost combination of foods that meets all daily nutritional requirements.
diet_optimization.description: Select food quantities to minimize total daily food cost while satisfying minimum requirements for calories, protein, calcium, fiber, and other nutrients. Each food has a per-unit cost and a nutritional profile. The solver outputs the optimal daily portions. This is the classic linear programming diet problem, useful for meal planning, institutional catering, and humanitarian food-aid logistics.
diet_optimization.scenarioDescription: A university cafeteria planner must design a daily menu using 5 staple items (bread, milk, eggs, chicken, rice). Each item has a known cost per serving and nutrient content. The menu must provide at least 2,000 kcal, 50 g protein, 800 mg calcium, and 25 g fiber at the lowest possible cost.

drug_distribution.shortDescription: Route pharmaceutical shipments from warehouses to pharmacies and hospitals to minimize logistics cost with temperature constraints.
drug_distribution.description: Plans the distribution of pharmaceutical products from regional warehouses to pharmacies and hospital dispensaries. Each product has temperature requirements (cold-chain, ambient) that restrict which vehicles and routes can carry it. The model minimizes total transportation cost while ensuring each destination receives its required quantities on time and cold-chain integrity is maintained throughout. Helps pharma distributors plan daily delivery routes.
drug_distribution.scenarioDescription: A pharmaceutical distributor operates two warehouses and must deliver vaccines (cold-chain), tablets, and insulin (cold-chain) to five destinations. Two refrigerated vans and one standard van are available. Vaccines and insulin require cold-chain vehicles. The logistics manager wants to plan routes that deliver everything on time at minimum cost.

drug_trial_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule clinical trial phases across sites and time periods to minimize time-to-market while respecting regulatory and resource constraints.
drug_trial_scheduling.description: Plans the execution of clinical trial phases across multiple hospital sites and time periods. Each trial phase has patient enrollment requirements, duration, prerequisite phases, and site qualification constraints. The model minimizes total trial duration (time-to-market) while ensuring each site does not exceed its patient enrollment capacity and all regulatory sequencing requirements are met. Helps pharmaceutical companies accelerate drug development pipelines.
drug_trial_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A biotech company is planning a new oncology drug through four trial phases. Phase I (safety) must complete before Phase II (efficacy), which must finish before two parallel Phase III pivotal studies. Five qualified hospital sites each have limited monthly patient enrollment capacity. The company wants to reach FDA submission as quickly as possible within a four-year window.

dye_batch_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule fabric dyeing batches to minimize color changeover costs and water usage while meeting delivery deadlines.
dye_batch_scheduling.description: Sequences dyeing jobs on dye machines to minimize total changeover cost. Changing from a dark color to a light one requires extensive machine cleaning, while light-to-dark transitions are cheaper. Each job has a fabric volume, color, processing time, and delivery deadline. The model assigns jobs to machines and determines their sequence to minimize changeover and cleaning costs while completing all jobs on time.
dye_batch_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A textile dyeing plant has two jet-dye machines running 16-hour shifts. Six dyeing jobs in different colors must be completed by their deadlines. The plant wants to sequence jobs to minimize changeover cost -- switching from dark to light colors requires a 90-minute cleaning cycle, while light-to-dark needs only 30 minutes.

emergency_response_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate emergency response units to zones to ensure every area is covered within response time targets.
emergency_response_allocation.description: Positions emergency response units (ambulances, fire engines, police cars) across stations to ensure every zone in the jurisdiction can be reached within a target response time. Each station has a capacity for units, and each zone has a demand rate for emergencies. The model minimizes total deployment cost while ensuring coverage requirements are met -- every zone must be reachable by at least one unit within the response time, and high-demand zones need backup coverage. A set-covering formulation used by emergency management agencies.
emergency_response_allocation.scenarioDescription: A city emergency services department has five stations and needs to deploy ambulance units across six response zones. The CBD zone (Zone A) has the highest call volume and requires two-unit coverage. Suburban zones need at least one unit within ten minutes. The department wants to position the minimum number of units across stations to meet all coverage requirements at the lowest annual cost.

emission_reduction_planning.shortDescription: Select emission reduction projects to meet a CO2 target at minimum total investment cost.
emission_reduction_planning.description: Choose from a portfolio of emission reduction initiatives (energy efficiency, renewable installations, process changes) to meet a corporate or regulatory CO2 reduction target at minimum total cost. Each initiative has an implementation cost, an annual CO2 reduction, and optional dependencies. The solver picks the cheapest combination that achieves the target. Useful for corporate sustainability planning and government climate policy.
emission_reduction_planning.scenarioDescription: A manufacturing company must reduce annual CO2 emissions by 500 tonnes to meet new regulations. Eight candidate projects range from LED lighting (EUR 5k, 30t reduction) to a solar installation (EUR 80k, 200t reduction). The sustainability officer wants the cheapest plan that hits the target.

employee_scheduling.shortDescription: Create optimal shift schedules that meet coverage requirements while minimizing labour cost and respecting availability.
employee_scheduling.description: Build weekly shift rosters for a team of employees. The solver assigns employees to shifts to minimize total cost while ensuring every shift has adequate staffing. It respects maximum and minimum working hours per employee, unavailability windows, and shift-capacity limits. The output is a ready-to-post schedule showing who works which shift.
employee_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A bakery has 5 employees covering 7 shifts across the week. Each shift needs 1-3 staff. Alice earns EUR 25/h but cannot work Saturday night. Bob is cheaper at EUR 22/h. The manager wants the cheapest schedule that covers every shift.

energy_storage_dispatch.shortDescription: Schedule when to charge and discharge a battery system to maximize arbitrage profit from time-of-use electricity prices.
energy_storage_dispatch.description: Optimize the charge/discharge schedule of a battery energy storage system across a day with variable electricity prices. The solver decides when to buy cheap off-peak power to charge and when to sell or use stored energy during expensive peak hours. Constraints include battery capacity, max charge/discharge rates, and round-trip efficiency losses. The output is an hourly charge/discharge plan and expected profit.
energy_storage_dispatch.scenarioDescription: A commercial building has a 500 kWh battery (max charge/discharge 100 kW, 90% round-trip efficiency). Electricity costs EUR 0.08/kWh overnight, EUR 0.15/kWh mid-day, and EUR 0.25/kWh at peak (17:00-20:00). The facility manager wants to maximize savings by shifting load to off-peak hours.

equipment_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate heavy equipment to construction sites to minimize rental costs and idle time.
equipment_allocation.description: Assigns pieces of heavy equipment (excavators, cranes, loaders) to construction sites over a planning horizon. Each site has time-varying equipment needs. Equipment can be moved between sites at a transportation cost, or additional units can be rented from external suppliers. The model minimizes total cost (owned equipment transport + rental fees + idle costs) while ensuring every site has the equipment it needs when it needs it.
equipment_allocation.scenarioDescription: A construction company has five pieces of heavy equipment spread across two active sites and a depot. Three sites need specific equipment next week. Moving equipment between sites costs money, and idle equipment incurs storage fees. The fleet manager needs to assign equipment to sites at minimum total cost.

fabric_cutting.shortDescription: Minimize fabric waste by optimizing cutting patterns for garment pieces across fabric rolls.
fabric_cutting.description: Determines how to cut garment pattern pieces from fabric rolls to minimize total waste (trim loss). Each roll has a fixed width and length. Each garment order requires specific pattern pieces in certain quantities. The model selects cutting patterns and their repetition counts to fulfill all orders using the fewest rolls possible. A direct application of the cutting stock problem used by textile and garment manufacturers worldwide.
fabric_cutting.scenarioDescription: A garment factory is cutting fabric for a shirt production run. The fabric comes in 150 cm wide rolls. Seven different pattern pieces (front panels in S/M/L, sleeves in S/M/L, and collars) must be cut in specific quantities. The cutting room supervisor wants to minimize the number of fabric rolls used by finding the best combination of cutting patterns.

fertilizer_mixing.shortDescription: Blend raw materials into a fertilizer mix that meets nutrient targets (N-P-K) at minimum cost.
fertilizer_mixing.description: Optimize a fertilizer blend by choosing quantities of raw materials so that the final mix meets minimum and maximum nutrient percentages for nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and any other tracked nutrients. The solver minimizes total raw-material cost while respecting supplier stock limits and batch-size constraints. The output tells you exactly how many tonnes of each ingredient to purchase.
fertilizer_mixing.scenarioDescription: An agri-cooperative needs to produce 1,000 kg of NPK fertilizer. Four raw materials are available -- urea (46% N), DAP (18% N, 46% P), MOP (60% K), and inert filler. Target blend: 15-20% N, 10-15% P, 12-18% K. Prices range from EUR 50 (filler) to EUR 480 (DAP) per tonne.

fleet_dispatch_mining.shortDescription: Dispatch haul trucks between shovels and dumps to maximize production throughput.
fleet_dispatch_mining.description: Assigns haul trucks to shovel-dump routes in an open-pit mine to maximize total tonnes moved per shift. Each shovel has a loading rate, each dump has a receiving capacity, and each truck has a payload and cycle time that depends on the route. The model decides how many truck trips to allocate to each shovel-dump pair to maximize production while balancing shovel utilization and avoiding truck queuing at any point.
fleet_dispatch_mining.scenarioDescription: An open-pit copper mine operates three shovels and dispatches material to a crusher and a waste dump. Twelve haul trucks are available for the shift. The dispatch optimizer determines how to route trucks between shovels and dumps to move the maximum tonnage while keeping shovels busy and avoiding bottlenecks at the crusher.

fleet_sizing.shortDescription: Determine the optimal number and type of vehicles to operate for a set of delivery routes and demand levels.
fleet_sizing.description: Decide how many vehicles of each type (small van, medium truck, large truck) to deploy to cover a set of demand segments at minimum total cost. Each vehicle type has a capacity, a fixed daily cost, and a variable cost per unit delivered. The solver ensures total fleet capacity meets demand while minimizing the sum of fixed and variable costs. The result tells you the fleet mix and which segments each vehicle type covers.
fleet_sizing.scenarioDescription: A courier company serves 5 city zones with daily parcel volumes of 50-300 parcels. Three vehicle types are available: bikes (20 parcels, EUR 30/day), vans (80 parcels, EUR 90/day), and trucks (200 parcels, EUR 180/day). The logistics manager wants the cheapest fleet that handles peak demand.

flight_crew_pairing.shortDescription: Construct minimum-cost crew pairings that cover all scheduled flight legs while respecting duty-time regulations.
flight_crew_pairing.description: Builds crew duty pairings (sequences of flight legs starting and ending at a crew base) that cover every scheduled flight at minimum cost. Each pairing must comply with maximum duty hours, minimum rest periods, and connection time limits. The model selects a set of pairings from candidates so that every flight leg is covered exactly once. A classic set-covering problem that airlines solve monthly for schedule planning.
flight_crew_pairing.scenarioDescription: A regional airline needs to build crew pairings for next month's schedule of eight flight legs across six cities. Eight candidate pairings have been enumerated, each with different costs (driven by hotel, per-diem, and positioning expenses). Every flight leg must be covered by exactly one pairing while minimizing total crew cost.

harvest_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule timber harvesting across forest stands over multiple periods to maximize revenue while ensuring sustainability.
harvest_scheduling.description: Plans which forest stands to harvest in each period of a multi-year horizon. Each stand has a timber volume, species mix, age, and expected revenue at harvest. The model maximizes total discounted revenue while respecting sustainability constraints -- maximum area harvested per period, adjacency rules (no two neighboring stands harvested in the same period), and minimum ending forest inventory. Fundamental for forest management companies and public land agencies.
harvest_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A forestry company manages 158 hectares across six stands of Douglas fir and spruce. Over a four-year plan, no more than 40 hectares may be harvested per year to comply with sustainability regulations. Older stands yield higher-value timber. The company wants to maximize total harvest revenue while maintaining sustainable practices.

ingredient_sourcing.shortDescription: Select suppliers and order quantities for food ingredients to minimize procurement cost while ensuring quality and delivery reliability.
ingredient_sourcing.description: Decides how much of each ingredient to order from each supplier. Suppliers offer different prices, minimum order quantities, lead times, and quality certifications. The model minimizes total procurement cost (purchase plus freight) while meeting demand for each ingredient, respecting supplier capacity, and ensuring a minimum number of qualified backup suppliers per critical ingredient for supply chain resilience.
ingredient_sourcing.scenarioDescription: A chocolate manufacturer needs to source three key ingredients for next quarter's production. Multiple suppliers offer different prices and capacities. Each supplier has minimum order requirements. The procurement manager wants to find the cheapest sourcing plan that meets all ingredient demands while respecting supplier constraints.

inventory_optimization.shortDescription: Determine optimal reorder quantities and safety stock levels to minimize holding and shortage costs.
inventory_optimization.description: Decide how much of each product to order and when, balancing inventory holding costs against stockout penalties. The solver considers demand forecasts, lead times, storage capacity, and supplier minimum order quantities to produce an ordering plan that minimizes total supply chain cost. The output tells you order quantities per period and expected inventory levels.
inventory_optimization.scenarioDescription: A consumer electronics distributor manages 5 product lines with weekly demand between 50 and 300 units. Warehouse capacity is 2,000 units total. Holding cost is EUR 0.50/unit/week and stockout penalty is EUR 5/unit. Lead time is 2 weeks. The supply chain manager wants a 6-week ordering plan.

inventory_replenishment.shortDescription: Plan when and how much to reorder for each SKU to minimize total holding and ordering costs.
inventory_replenishment.description: Determines the optimal reorder quantities and timing for warehouse SKUs over a planning horizon. Each SKU has a forecasted demand, holding cost per unit per period, fixed ordering cost, and lead time. The model minimizes total cost (ordering + holding + potential stockout penalties) while ensuring service level targets are met. A multi-item lot-sizing problem that warehouse managers solve weekly or monthly.
inventory_replenishment.scenarioDescription: An office supplies warehouse manages four SKU categories over a six-week horizon. Each SKU has weekly demand forecasts that vary. Placing an order incurs a fixed cost regardless of quantity, and holding inventory costs money each week. The inventory planner wants to decide when to place orders and how much to order to minimize total costs while meeting all demand.

irrigation_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule water distribution across fields and time slots to minimize water usage while meeting crop requirements.
irrigation_scheduling.description: Determine how much water to deliver to each field in each time slot to satisfy crop water requirements at minimum total usage. The solver considers pump capacity, pipe flow limits, and crop-specific minimum hydration levels. The result is a time-based irrigation schedule that avoids both over-watering and crop stress.
irrigation_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A farm has 4 fields with different crops and 6 daily irrigation slots. The main pump delivers 120 m3/hour. Field A (tomatoes) needs 40 m3/day; field B (lettuce) needs 25 m3/day. The farmer wants to minimize total water consumption while keeping every crop above its minimum hydration threshold.

job_shop_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule jobs across machines to minimize total completion time (makespan) while respecting processing order constraints.
job_shop_scheduling.description: Assign start times to operations of multiple jobs on multiple machines so that the overall completion time (makespan) is minimized. Each job has a sequence of operations, each requiring a specific machine for a specific duration. No two operations can use the same machine simultaneously. The solver produces a Gantt- chart-ready schedule showing when each operation starts and on which machine.
job_shop_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A metal shop has 3 machines (lathe, mill, drill). Four customer orders each require 2-3 machining operations in a fixed sequence. Processing times range from 1 to 5 hours. The shop foreman needs a schedule that finishes all orders as early as possible.

knapsack.shortDescription: Select the best combination of items to maximize value while staying within a single capacity limit.
knapsack.description: The classic 0-1 knapsack problem: given a set of items each with a value and a weight, choose which items to pack so that total value is maximized without exceeding the container's weight capacity. Every item is either fully included or excluded. Use this for cargo loading, project selection, or any scenario where you pick from a list of indivisible options under a single resource constraint.
knapsack.scenarioDescription: A traveller is packing a 50 kg suitcase for an international trip. Eight items compete for space -- a laptop, camera, headphones, book, tablet, charger, clothes, and shoes. Each item has a subjective value score and a weight. The goal is to maximise total value within the weight limit.

launch_vehicle_payload.shortDescription: Select and arrange satellite payloads for a launch vehicle to maximize total mission value within mass and volume limits.
launch_vehicle_payload.description: Decides which satellite payloads to include on a rideshare launch mission. Each payload has a mass, volume, an orbital destination preference, and a revenue value. The launch vehicle has a maximum payload mass and fairing volume. The model maximizes total mission revenue while respecting mass and volume constraints and ensuring orbital compatibility between co-manifested payloads. Used by launch service providers for rideshare mission planning.
launch_vehicle_payload.scenarioDescription: A launch provider is planning a rideshare mission to Sun-synchronous orbit. Six payloads have requested manifest slots. The rocket can carry 500 kg and the fairing has 4.0 m3 of usable volume. The mission planner needs to select payloads that maximize total launch revenue within mass and volume limits.

loan_portfolio.shortDescription: Select which loan applications to approve to maximize interest income while staying within risk and capital limits.
loan_portfolio.description: A bank or lender receives multiple loan applications and must decide which to approve. Each loan has an expected interest income, a risk score, and a capital requirement. The solver maximises total expected income subject to a total capital budget, a maximum average risk threshold, and optional sector diversification rules. The result tells you which loans to approve and the overall portfolio metrics.
loan_portfolio.scenarioDescription: A regional bank has EUR 2M in available lending capital and 10 loan applications ranging from EUR 50k to EUR 500k. Each loan has a projected annual interest rate and a risk rating from 1 (low) to 5 (high). The board wants average portfolio risk below 3.0.

markdown_pricing.shortDescription: Set optimal clearance prices for end-of-season inventory to maximize total revenue while clearing stock.
markdown_pricing.description: Determine the markdown percentage for each product in an end-of-season clearance to maximize total revenue. Each product has remaining inventory, a base price, and a demand elasticity estimate (how many more units sell per percentage discount). The solver balances deep discounts that move volume against shallow discounts that preserve margin. Useful for seasonal retail, fashion, and perishable goods.
markdown_pricing.scenarioDescription: A fashion retailer has 8 end-of-season items with 20-200 units remaining. Base prices range from EUR 25 to EUR 150. The merchandiser estimates demand elasticity from past sales. The goal is to clear at least 80% of inventory while maximising total clearance revenue.

material_procurement.shortDescription: Select suppliers and order quantities for building materials to minimize total procurement cost with delivery constraints.
material_procurement.description: Decides which suppliers to use and how much of each building material to order from each. Suppliers have different prices, minimum order quantities, delivery lead times, and quality ratings. The model minimizes total cost (material + delivery + handling) while ensuring all material requirements are met on time for the construction schedule and minimum quality thresholds are maintained.
material_procurement.scenarioDescription: A construction project manager needs to procure concrete, rebar, and lumber for a new building. Two suppliers offer each material at different prices and capacities. Some suppliers have lower unit costs but higher delivery charges. The manager wants to find the cheapest combination of suppliers that covers all material requirements.

max_flow.shortDescription: Push the maximum amount of flow from source to sink through a capacitated network.
max_flow.description: Find the maximum flow that can be pushed from a source node to a sink node in a directed network where each arc has a capacity limit. The solver determines how much flow goes through each arc while respecting capacity constraints and flow conservation at every intermediate node. Applications include network bandwidth allocation, pipeline throughput, and evacuation planning.
max_flow.scenarioDescription: A water utility must determine the maximum flow from a reservoir (source) to a treatment plant (sink) through a pipe network with 5 intermediate junctions. Each pipe has a maximum flow capacity in litres per second. The engineer wants to know the network's bottleneck throughput.

media_mix_optimization.shortDescription: Find the optimal media spending mix across platforms to maximize ROI given diminishing returns and cross-channel effects.
media_mix_optimization.description: Determines the optimal allocation of marketing spend across media platforms considering diminishing returns on each channel. Each platform has a budget range, an expected return curve, and synergy effects with other platforms (e.g., TV ads boost search conversion). The model maximizes total marketing ROI while staying within the overall budget and respecting per-channel minimum and maximum constraints. A modern take on the classic Markowitz portfolio approach applied to marketing.
media_mix_optimization.scenarioDescription: A DTC brand has $80,000 monthly to spend across six media channels. Each channel has a different expected ROI and variance (risk that actual returns deviate from expected). The marketing team wants a diversified media mix that maximizes expected return while controlling overall campaign risk.

mine_production_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule extraction blocks across time periods to maximize net present value while meeting blending and capacity constraints.
mine_production_scheduling.description: Determines when to extract each ore block from an open-pit mine over a multi-period horizon. Each block has a tonnage, grade, and extraction cost. The model maximizes the net present value of the extracted ore while respecting mining precedence (upper blocks before lower), processing plant capacity, and minimum ore grade requirements per period. A fundamental problem in strategic mine planning.
mine_production_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A copper mine has six ore blocks across three bench levels. The processing plant can handle 10,000 tonnes per year. Upper-level blocks must be extracted before lower ones. The mine planner needs to decide which blocks to extract in each of three years to maximize the total net present value of the extracted copper ore.

minimum_spanning_tree.shortDescription: Connect all nodes in a network at minimum total edge cost without cycles.
minimum_spanning_tree.description: Select edges to connect all nodes in a graph at minimum total cost, forming a spanning tree (connected, acyclic subgraph). Each edge has a weight (cost, distance, or time). The solver uses an IP formulation with connectivity constraints to find the MST. Useful for designing cable networks, pipeline systems, and road networks where every location must be connected.
minimum_spanning_tree.scenarioDescription: A utility company needs to connect 6 villages with fibre-optic cable. Laying cable between each pair has a different cost depending on terrain. The company wants every village connected at minimum total cable cost.

multi_modal_freight.shortDescription: Choose the best combination of transport modes (road, rail, sea) for each shipment leg to minimize total cost and time.
multi_modal_freight.description: Plan freight shipments across a network that offers multiple transport modes (truck, rail, air, sea) on each leg. Each mode has a different cost per tonne-km, transit time, capacity, and carbon footprint. The solver selects the optimal mode for each leg and the volume to ship, minimizing total cost while meeting delivery deadlines and capacity constraints. Useful for international logistics planning.
multi_modal_freight.scenarioDescription: A manufacturer ships goods from a factory to 3 export ports, with 2 intermediate hubs. Truck, rail, and barge options exist on each leg with varying cost, time, and CO2. The total shipment is 500 tonnes with a 5-day deadline. The logistics team wants the cheapest plan that meets the deadline.

multi_objective_demo.shortDescription: Explore trade-offs between competing objectives using weighted-sum scalarization.
multi_objective_demo.description: A teaching template that demonstrates how to handle multiple conflicting objectives by combining them into a single weighted sum. Define two or more objectives, assign weights, and the solver finds the Pareto-optimal solution for that weight combination. Run with different weights to trace the Pareto frontier. Useful for learning about multi-criteria decision-making and trade-off analysis.
multi_objective_demo.scenarioDescription: A city planner must balance cost minimization and environmental impact minimization when choosing infrastructure investments. Three projects are available, each with different cost, environmental impact, and community benefit scores. Weights let the planner explore the trade-off.

network_redundancy.shortDescription: Design a minimum-cost redundant network ensuring every node has at least two independent paths.
network_redundancy.description: Select edges from a candidate set to build a communication network where every pair of critical nodes is connected by at least two edge-disjoint paths. Each candidate edge has an installation cost. The model minimizes total cost while guaranteeing fault tolerance -- if any single link fails, all nodes remain reachable. Essential for designing resilient fiber-optic or microwave backhaul networks.
network_redundancy.scenarioDescription: A regional ISP is building a fiber backbone connecting its data center to five hub sites through a central exchange. Nine candidate fiber routes have been surveyed. The ISP requires that every hub has at least two independent paths to the data center so that a single fiber cut does not cause an outage.

nurse_scheduling.shortDescription: Create weekly nurse shift schedules that meet ward coverage requirements while respecting labour rules and preferences.
nurse_scheduling.description: Assign nurses to shifts across a week so that every ward has adequate staffing at all times. The solver minimizes total labour cost (or maximizes preference satisfaction) while enforcing minimum rest between shifts, maximum hours per week, and unavailability windows. The output is a ready-to-post weekly roster that balances fairness and cost.
nurse_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A hospital ward has 8 nurses covering 14 shifts per week (morning and evening, 7 days). Each shift needs 2-3 nurses. Nurses have maximum 40-hour weeks, at least 10 hours between shifts, and some have day-off requests. The head nurse wants the cheapest compliant schedule.

one_d_cutting_stock.shortDescription: Cut standard-length stock material into customer orders to minimize total waste.
one_d_cutting_stock.description: Determine how to cut standard-length rolls, bars, or boards into customer-ordered lengths to minimize total material waste. The solver enumerates feasible cutting patterns (combinations of ordered lengths that fit in one stock unit) and decides how many times to use each pattern. The output shows which patterns to cut and how many stock units are needed. Classic application for paper mills, steel bars, and lumber.
one_d_cutting_stock.scenarioDescription: A steel supplier cuts 12-metre standard bars into 5 customer order lengths (2m, 3m, 4m, 5m, 7m) with varying quantities needed. Scrap from each bar is waste. The production manager wants to minimize the number of bars used.

operating_room_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule surgeries across operating rooms and time blocks to maximize throughput while respecting surgeon and equipment availability.
operating_room_scheduling.description: Assign surgical cases to operating rooms and time blocks to maximize the number of completed surgeries (or minimize idle time). Each surgery has a duration, a required surgeon, and equipment needs. The solver ensures no room or surgeon is double-booked and that all mandatory cases are scheduled within the planning window. Useful for hospital operations managers.
operating_room_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A hospital has 3 operating rooms available for 5 daily time blocks (08:00-16:00 in 90-minute slots). Ten elective surgeries of varying durations (45-180 min) are pending. Three surgeons are available with different schedules. The surgical coordinator wants to fit as many cases as possible into tomorrow's schedule.

ore_blending.shortDescription: Blend ore from multiple sources to meet quality specifications at minimum cost.
ore_blending.description: Determines the optimal blend of ore from different stockpiles or mine faces to produce a product that meets customer quality specifications. Each source has known concentrations of valuable minerals and impurities. The model minimizes blending and processing cost while ensuring the final product stays within acceptable ranges for all quality parameters (e.g., iron content, silica, moisture). Used daily at processing plants and ports.
ore_blending.scenarioDescription: An iron ore mine ships 15,000 tonnes per week to a port customer who requires at least 62% iron, no more than 5% silica, and maximum 8.5% moisture. The mine has four ore sources with different qualities and handling costs. The blend planner needs to find the cheapest mix that meets all specifications.

pick_route_optimization.shortDescription: Optimize the sequence of picks in a warehouse order to minimize total travel distance.
pick_route_optimization.description: Determines the shortest route for a warehouse picker to visit all required storage locations for an order and return to the packing station. Each location has coordinates in the warehouse grid and an aisle/level. The model finds the visit sequence that minimizes total walking distance, considering aisle layout and one-way travel restrictions. Applies to any order-picking warehouse operation, especially for large multi-item orders.
pick_route_optimization.scenarioDescription: A picker in an e-commerce warehouse needs to collect items from six different locations across four aisles and return to the packing station. The warehouse layout creates varying distances between locations. The pick route optimizer finds the shortest sequence of visits to minimize total walking distance for this order.

pipeline_network_flow.shortDescription: Route chemical products through a pipeline network to minimize pumping costs while meeting delivery requirements.
pipeline_network_flow.description: Determines flow rates through a chemical pipeline network connecting production plants to storage terminals and customers. Each pipeline segment has a capacity and a pumping cost per unit flow. The model minimizes total transport cost while ensuring every customer node receives its contracted volume and no pipeline exceeds its rated capacity. Applicable to petrochemical distribution, natural gas networks, and water treatment systems.
pipeline_network_flow.scenarioDescription: A petrochemical company distributes products from two refineries to three industrial customers through a pipeline network with two tank farm junctions. Pumping costs vary by segment. The distribution planner needs to route 8,000 barrels per day through the network at minimum cost.

port_berth_allocation.shortDescription: Assign arriving vessels to port berths to minimize total waiting and handling time.
port_berth_allocation.description: Schedules vessel arrivals to available berths at a container port. Each vessel has an expected arrival time, a handling duration (based on cargo volume), a draft requirement, and a preferred berth. Each berth has a depth limit and crane availability. The model minimizes total vessel turnaround time (waiting + handling) while preventing berth conflicts and ensuring draft compatibility. A key operational planning problem for port authorities.
port_berth_allocation.scenarioDescription: A container terminal has three berths of varying depth and crane availability. Five container vessels are expected to arrive over a 10-hour window, each with different draft requirements and cargo volumes. The port planner needs to assign vessels to berths and schedule service times to minimize total vessel turnaround time.

portfolio_optimization.shortDescription: Build an investment portfolio that maximizes expected return for a given risk tolerance, with optional cardinality and sector limits.
portfolio_optimization.description: Construct an optimal investment portfolio by choosing allocations across assets that maximise expected return while keeping portfolio risk below a threshold. Supports minimum and maximum allocation per asset, cardinality constraints (limit the number of assets held), and sector-level caps. The solution tells you exactly how much to invest in each asset and what overall return and risk to expect.
portfolio_optimization.scenarioDescription: An investment advisor manages a EUR 100,000 portfolio with six asset classes: S&P 500 ETF, Euro bonds, a tech ETF, real estate, gold, and emerging markets. The client wants at least 7% annual return but no more than 15% portfolio risk, with at most 60% in equities.

power_generation_mix.shortDescription: Determine the optimal mix of power plants to operate each hour to meet demand at minimum fuel and emission cost.
power_generation_mix.description: Schedule the output of multiple power generators (gas, coal, solar, wind, nuclear) across time periods to meet hourly electricity demand at minimum total cost. Each generator has a capacity range, a per-MWh fuel cost, a ramp rate, and CO2 emissions. The solver decides how much each plant produces each hour while respecting minimum up-time, ramp limits, and an optional emissions cap. The result is a dispatch schedule and total cost breakdown.
power_generation_mix.scenarioDescription: A regional grid operator manages 5 generators: a gas turbine (50-200 MW, EUR 45/MWh), a coal plant (100-300 MW, EUR 30/MWh), solar (0-80 MW, EUR 0/MWh but intermittent), wind (0-120 MW, EUR 0/MWh), and a peaker (0-100 MW, EUR 70/MWh). Demand peaks at 450 MW during hour 14 and dips to 200 MW overnight. The operator wants the cheapest dispatch plan for a 24-hour window.

production_batch_planning.shortDescription: Plan production batches of multiple drug products to meet demand while minimizing setup costs and waste.
production_batch_planning.description: Schedules production batches of pharmaceutical products across reactors and time periods. Each product has a demand forecast, batch size, production time, and shelf life. Reactor changeovers between different products incur cleaning validation costs and downtime. The model minimizes total production plus setup cost while meeting all demand and ensuring no batch expires before delivery. Applies to any GMP manufacturing facility.
production_batch_planning.scenarioDescription: A pharmaceutical plant operates two reactors to produce three drug products over a four-week horizon. Each product has weekly demand that must be met from production or inventory. Switching a reactor between products requires expensive cleaning validation. The production planner needs to decide how many batches of each product to produce each week to minimize total cost.

production_line_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule food products across production lines to minimize changeover times and meet daily order deadlines.
production_line_scheduling.description: Assigns food product orders to production lines and sequences them to minimize total changeover (cleaning) time. Changeover duration depends on the product pair (allergen cleaning takes longer). Each order has a volume, a production rate, and a delivery deadline. The model ensures all orders are completed on time, lines do not exceed their daily capacity, and allergen contamination rules are respected.
production_line_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A snack factory runs two production lines for 16 hours each. Six orders for different products must be completed by their deadlines. Switching between certain products (especially those with allergen concerns like peanut butter) requires lengthy cleaning. The scheduler needs to assign and sequence orders to minimize total changeover time while meeting all deadlines.

production_planning.shortDescription: Determine optimal production quantities for multiple products to maximize profit under resource constraints.
production_planning.description: Decide how many units of each product to manufacture in order to maximize total profit. Each product consumes shared resources (raw materials, labour hours, machine time) and has a per-unit profit margin plus optional minimum and maximum production bounds. The solver produces a production plan that respects every resource limit while maximising the bottom line.
production_planning.scenarioDescription: A furniture workshop produces chairs, tables, and desks. Each product requires wood, labour hours, and screws in different quantities. Wood is limited to 500 units, labour to 200 hours, and screws to 1,000. The shop owner wants to know how many of each item to make this month.

project_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule construction activities across trades to minimize project duration while respecting dependencies and resource limits.
project_scheduling.description: Plans the start time of each construction activity to minimize total project duration (make-span). Activities have durations, precedence constraints (foundation before framing, framing before roofing), and resource requirements (crane hours, skilled labor). The model ensures no resource is over-allocated in any period and all dependencies are satisfied. Produces a resource-leveled schedule that general contractors use for project planning.
project_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A general contractor is building a commercial structure with nine major activities from site preparation through finishing. Ten laborers and one crane are available daily. Activities have strict sequencing requirements (cannot drywall before rough plumbing is complete). The contractor wants the shortest possible project schedule that respects all dependencies and resource limits.

property_portfolio.shortDescription: Select the best mix of properties to maximize risk-adjusted returns within a capital budget.
property_portfolio.description: Builds an optimal real estate investment portfolio by choosing which properties to acquire from a list of candidates. Each property has an expected annual return, a risk score, and a purchase price. The model maximizes total expected return while staying within the investment budget and respecting diversification constraints across property types (residential, commercial, industrial). Ideal for REITs and institutional investors.
property_portfolio.scenarioDescription: A real estate investment trust has $8M to deploy across eight candidate properties spanning residential, commercial, and industrial segments. Each property has a different yield, risk profile, and acquisition cost. The fund manager wants to maximize expected returns while keeping overall portfolio risk manageable.

public_budget_allocation.shortDescription: Allocate a government budget across programs and departments to maximize public benefit within fiscal constraints.
public_budget_allocation.description: Distributes a fixed annual budget across government programs (education, healthcare, infrastructure, public safety, social services). Each program has an estimated benefit score per dollar spent, a minimum funding floor, and a maximum absorption capacity. The model maximizes total weighted public benefit while meeting minimum funding requirements for all programs and staying within the total budget. Supports participatory budgeting and evidence-based fiscal planning.
public_budget_allocation.scenarioDescription: A city government has a $40 million annual budget to allocate across seven departments. Each department has a minimum funding requirement and a maximum it can absorb. Independent evaluations have estimated the social benefit per dollar for each program. The city council wants an allocation that maximizes total public benefit within the budget.

public_facility_location.shortDescription: Determine optimal locations for public facilities (schools, hospitals, fire stations) to maximize population coverage equity.
public_facility_location.description: Selects sites for new public facilities from candidate locations to ensure equitable access for all communities. Each candidate site has a construction cost and serves surrounding population zones within a distance threshold. The model minimizes total cost (or maximizes coverage) while ensuring no community is farther than a maximum acceptable distance from at least one facility. Incorporates equity constraints so that underserved areas are prioritized. Used by municipal planners for schools, clinics, fire stations, and libraries.
public_facility_location.scenarioDescription: A city council has budget to build three new community health clinics. Five candidate sites have been identified across the city, each with different construction costs and capacities. Six residential communities need service, with three currently designated as underserved. The planning department wants to choose three sites that maximize population coverage, prioritizing underserved communities.

quality_control_sampling.shortDescription: Choose how many items to sample from each production batch to meet defect-detection targets at minimum inspection cost.
quality_control_sampling.description: Determine the optimal number of units to inspect from each production batch to achieve a target defect detection rate while minimizing total inspection cost. Each batch has a known size, an estimated defect rate, and a per-unit inspection cost. The solver balances inspection effort against detection confidence.
quality_control_sampling.scenarioDescription: A food manufacturer runs 5 production lines, each producing batches of 500-2000 units per day. Historical defect rates vary from 0.5% to 3%. Quality assurance must detect at least 95% of defects across all lines with a budget of EUR 800 per day for inspections.

raw_material_purchasing.shortDescription: Decide how much of each raw material to buy from which supplier to meet production needs at lowest total cost.
raw_material_purchasing.description: Purchase raw materials from multiple suppliers to satisfy production requirements at minimum cost. Each supplier offers different prices, minimum order quantities, and maximum capacities. The solver determines the optimal order quantities per supplier per material, respecting all constraints. Useful for procurement planning and supplier negotiation.
raw_material_purchasing.scenarioDescription: A plastics manufacturer needs 3 types of resin for next month's production. Four suppliers offer different prices and lead times. Supplier A has a 5-tonne minimum order but the best price. The purchasing manager needs to meet demand of 20 tonnes total at the lowest cost.

reactor_optimization.shortDescription: Optimize feed rates and operating conditions across a reactor network to maximize product yield at minimum cost.
reactor_optimization.description: Determines optimal feed rates, temperatures, and residence times for a network of chemical reactors producing a target product. Each reactor has conversion rate parameters, capacity limits, and operating cost curves. The model maximizes net product output (or profit) while staying within safety limits on temperature and pressure. Raw material availability and product demand constraints complete the picture. Applicable to refineries, petrochemical plants, and specialty chemical producers.
reactor_optimization.scenarioDescription: A polymer plant operates three reactors (two CSTRs and one PFR) to convert ethylene and propylene into polyethylene and polypropylene. Each reactor has different throughput capacities and conversion efficiencies. Raw material supply is limited this quarter. The plant manager needs to allocate feeds across reactors to maximize profit while meeting minimum production targets.

recipe_optimization.shortDescription: Optimize ingredient proportions in food recipes to minimize cost while meeting nutritional and taste specifications.
recipe_optimization.description: Determines the cheapest mix of ingredients for a food product that satisfies nutritional requirements (calories, protein, fat, sodium, fiber), taste constraints, and regulatory labeling rules. Each ingredient has a cost per unit, nutritional profile, and availability limit. The model minimizes total ingredient cost per batch while ensuring the final product meets all quality specifications. Used by food manufacturers, bakeries, and pet food companies.
recipe_optimization.scenarioDescription: A granola bar manufacturer needs to produce a 500 kg batch. The product must meet specific nutritional targets (minimum protein, controlled fat and calories, minimum fiber). Six ingredients are available at different costs. The food scientist wants the cheapest formulation that satisfies all nutritional labeling requirements.

renewable_curtailment.shortDescription: Allocate grid capacity to renewable sources to minimize wasted (curtailed) clean energy.
renewable_curtailment.description: Allocate available grid transmission capacity among renewable generators (solar farms, wind farms) to minimize the amount of clean energy that must be curtailed (wasted) due to grid congestion. Each generator has a forecasted output profile and the grid has hourly transmission limits. The solver decides priority access so that total curtailment is minimized across the planning horizon.
renewable_curtailment.scenarioDescription: A grid region has 3 solar farms and 2 wind farms connected by transmission lines with a 400 MW combined limit. On a sunny, windy day, total renewable output could reach 550 MW. The grid operator needs to allocate capacity to minimize total MWh curtailed over 12 hours.

renovation_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule renovation tasks across multiple properties to minimize total project duration and contractor costs.
renovation_scheduling.description: Plans the sequence and timing of renovation activities across a portfolio of properties. Tasks have durations, precedence constraints, and share a limited pool of contractor crews.
