The model minimizes the total make-span (time to complete all renovations) or total cost, ensuring no crew is double-booked and all dependencies are respected. Helps property managers coordinate multi-site refurbishment programs efficiently.
renovation_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A property management company is renovating units in two apartment buildings simultaneously. Eight tasks must be completed across the two buildings, each with specific durations and dependencies (e.g., plumbing before painting). Only three contractor crews are available. The company wants to finish all renovations within 15 days.

reservoir_operation.shortDescription: Plan water releases from a reservoir over multiple periods to balance irrigation, power generation, and flood control.
reservoir_operation.description: Schedules water releases from a multi-purpose reservoir over a planning horizon (weeks or months). Inflows are forecasted, and the model must balance competing demands -- irrigation water for agriculture, minimum environmental flows, hydroelectric generation targets, and flood storage reserves. The model maximizes total benefit (irrigation value plus energy revenue) while keeping the reservoir level within safe operating bounds at all times.
reservoir_operation.scenarioDescription: A regional water authority manages a reservoir that serves both irrigation and hydroelectric purposes over a six-month dry-season horizon. Inflows peak in months 1-2 (rainy tail) and drop sharply by month 4. Irrigators downstream need increasing water as the dry season progresses. The authority must plan monthly releases that satisfy irrigation, generate electricity revenue, and keep the reservoir within safe operating limits.

revenue_maximization.shortDescription: Decide how many units of each product to sell across channels to maximize total revenue under capacity and demand limits.
revenue_maximization.description: Determine the optimal sales quantities for multiple products across multiple channels to maximize total revenue. Each product-channel pair has a unit price, a demand ceiling, and uses shared capacity resources. The solver finds the revenue-maximizing sales plan respecting all capacity limits. Useful for seasonal planning, product launch allocation, or multi-channel pricing decisions.
revenue_maximization.scenarioDescription: An electronics retailer sells 4 product lines (laptops, tablets, phones, accessories) through 3 channels (online, retail stores, wholesale). Each channel has limited fulfillment capacity and different margins. Total warehouse throughput is 5,000 units per week.

risk_pool_optimization.shortDescription: Construct an optimal insurance portfolio by selecting policies that maximize premium income while controlling aggregate risk exposure.
risk_pool_optimization.description: Selects which insurance policies to underwrite from a pool of applicants to maximize expected profit (premiums minus expected claims). Each policy has a premium, an expected loss ratio, a risk class, and a correlation factor with other policies. The model maximizes total expected profit while keeping aggregate Value-at-Risk below the company's risk tolerance and maintaining diversification across risk classes. Used by underwriters and reinsurance companies for portfolio construction.
risk_pool_optimization.scenarioDescription: A mid-size insurer is reviewing eight policy applications for the upcoming year. Each policy has a different premium, expected loss ratio, and risk class. The company's risk appetite limits total sum insured to $18 million. The underwriting team wants to select the most profitable mix of policies while staying within the aggregate risk budget.

rolling_stock_assignment.shortDescription: Assign train units to scheduled services to minimize empty repositioning while matching capacity to demand.
rolling_stock_assignment.description: Assigns available train units (rolling stock) to a set of scheduled services over a day or week. Each service has a passenger demand that determines the required train capacity. Train units can be coupled or split at certain stations. The model minimizes total deadheading (empty repositioning) cost and unmet demand while ensuring maintenance requirements are met and overnight parking capacity at depots is respected.
rolling_stock_assignment.scenarioDescription: Deutsche Bahn has five train units parked overnight at four stations and needs to assign them to five morning and afternoon services. Each service has an expected passenger count that determines the required seating capacity. The planner wants to minimize empty repositioning while ensuring no service runs with insufficient seats.

satellite_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule observation tasks across a satellite constellation to maximize completed requests within visibility windows.
satellite_scheduling.description: Assigns observation or communication tasks to satellites in a constellation during their visibility windows over target areas. Each task has a time window, a priority value, a duration, and specific satellite capability requirements. The model maximizes total priority-weighted task completion while respecting each satellite's energy budget, slew-time between targets, and memory/downlink constraints. Critical for Earth-observation and communications satellite operators.
satellite_scheduling.scenarioDescription: An Earth-observation company operates three satellites and has eight imaging requests for the next three-hour window. Requests range from emergency wildfire monitoring (high priority) to routine urban growth surveys. Each satellite has limited energy for imaging per orbit. The scheduler must assign tasks to satellites within their visibility windows to maximize the total priority score of completed observations.

service_center_placement.shortDescription: Select service center locations to minimize average customer travel time while staying within a budget for new openings.
service_center_placement.description: Choose which service centers to open (or keep open) from candidate locations so that the average travel time for customers is minimized. Each center has an opening/operating cost and each customer-center pair has a travel time. The solver respects a total budget constraint and ensures every customer is reachable. Useful for healthcare facility planning, government offices, and field service networks.
service_center_placement.scenarioDescription: A telecom company plans its field-service network. Six candidate locations are under consideration. Each has different lease costs and proximity to the 10 customer clusters. Budget allows opening at most 3 centers. The goal is to minimize average response time.

shortest_path.shortDescription: Find the least-cost path between two nodes in a network with weighted edges.
shortest_path.description: Find the minimum-cost path from a source node to a destination node in a directed or undirected graph. Each edge has a cost (distance, time, or monetary). The solver formulates this as a min-cost flow problem with unit flow and returns the optimal path and its total cost. Useful for route planning, network routing, and logistics path selection.
shortest_path.scenarioDescription: A delivery driver needs the cheapest route from the warehouse (node W) to customer site F through a road network of 7 intersections. Each road segment has a toll cost. The driver wants the route that minimises total toll.

store_layout.shortDescription: Assign product departments to store zones to maximize customer flow and impulse purchases.
store_layout.description: Place product departments into store zones to maximize a weighted customer-flow score. High-affinity departments (e.g., bakery and dairy) should be near each other to encourage cross-shopping. The solver considers zone sizes, department space requirements, and a pairwise affinity matrix. The result is an assignment map that maximizes expected revenue from impulse buys and logical adjacency.
store_layout.scenarioDescription: A supermarket is renovating and has 6 zones of varying sizes. Eight departments (produce, bakery, dairy, meat, frozen, beverages, snacks, household) need placement. The marketing team has a cross-shopping affinity matrix from loyalty card data. The store manager wants a layout that maximizes adjacent high-affinity pairs.

strip_packing.shortDescription: Pack rectangular items into a strip of fixed width to minimize the total height used.
strip_packing.description: Pack a set of rectangular items into a strip of fixed width, minimizing the total height (length) of material used. Items cannot overlap and cannot be rotated (or optionally can). This is modelled as a resource-constrained packing problem. Common in textile cutting, circuit board layout, and newspaper page design.
strip_packing.scenarioDescription: A fabric cutting machine has a 150 cm wide roll. Eight pattern pieces of varying widths (20-60 cm) and heights (10-40 cm) must be laid out. The goal is to minimize the total length of fabric consumed.

student_assignment.shortDescription: Assign students to course sections to maximize preference satisfaction while respecting section capacities.
student_assignment.description: Assign students to course sections when multiple sections of the same course are offered at different times. Each student ranks their preferred time slots. The solver maximizes total preference satisfaction (or minimizes dissatisfaction) while ensuring no section exceeds its capacity and every student gets exactly one section. Used by university registrars and training coordinators.
student_assignment.scenarioDescription: Forty students must be assigned to 4 sections of an introductory programming course (10 seats each). Each student has ranked the sections 1-4 by preference. The registrar wants an assignment that maximizes overall student satisfaction.

supplier_selection.shortDescription: Choose suppliers and allocate order quantities to minimize total procurement cost while meeting quality and delivery requirements.
supplier_selection.description: Select from a pool of suppliers and decide how much to order from each, minimizing total cost (unit price + shipping + quality-failure cost). Each supplier has capacity limits, minimum order sizes, quality ratings, and lead times. The solver ensures total demand is met while respecting diversification rules (e.g., no more than 60% from a single supplier). The output is an allocation plan per supplier.
supplier_selection.scenarioDescription: A bicycle manufacturer needs 10,000 frames per quarter from 5 candidate suppliers. Prices range from EUR 35-48 per frame. Supplier A is cheapest but has a 3% defect rate and 4-week lead time. Supplier D is most expensive but offers 0.5% defects and 1-week delivery. The procurement team wants the cost-optimal mix with less than 2% average defect rate.

team_roster_optimization.shortDescription: Select players for a team roster to maximize overall skill rating within salary cap and position constraints.
team_roster_optimization.description: Build the best possible sports roster by selecting players from a pool, maximizing total team skill rating while staying under a salary cap. Each player has a position, a salary, and a skill rating. The solver ensures positional requirements are met (e.g., 2 guards, 3 forwards, 1 center) and the total salary does not exceed the cap. Useful for fantasy sports, draft planning, and squad management.
team_roster_optimization.scenarioDescription: A fantasy basketball manager has a EUR 50,000 salary cap and must draft 8 players from a pool of 15 candidates. The team needs 2 guards, 3 forwards, 2 centers, and 1 utility player. Each player has a projected performance score and a salary. The goal is to maximise total projected points.

tenant_mix_optimization.shortDescription: Select tenants for a commercial property to maximize rental income and foot traffic synergy.
tenant_mix_optimization.description: Decides which tenant applicants to accept for available retail or office spaces in a commercial property. Each applicant offers a monthly rent, has a space requirement, a customer draw factor, and a category (food, fashion, services). The model maximizes total rental income plus synergy bonuses (complementary tenants attract more foot traffic) while avoiding category conflicts (no two direct competitors) and filling the available square footage optimally.
tenant_mix_optimization.scenarioDescription: A shopping center has 500 sqm of leasable space across ground-floor units. Eight tenant applicants want to move in, each with different rent offers, space requirements, and customer draw potential. The property manager wants to select tenants that maximize rental income and create a vibrant, high- traffic tenant mix.

timber_transportation.shortDescription: Route timber shipments from forest depots to mills to minimize total transportation cost.
timber_transportation.description: Determines how much timber to ship from each forest depot to each sawmill or pulp mill. Each depot has a supply of logs and each mill has a demand. Transportation costs vary by route distance and road quality. The model minimizes total freight cost while ensuring every mill receives enough timber and no depot ships more than it has available. A classic transportation problem applied to the forestry supply chain.
timber_transportation.scenarioDescription: A logging company has three forest depots with harvested timber that needs to reach three mills (two sawmills and one pulp mill). Each route has a different cost per cubic meter depending on distance and road conditions. The company must ship all demanded timber at minimum total freight cost.

tournament_scheduling.shortDescription: Create a round-robin tournament schedule that minimizes total travel or balances home/away games.
tournament_scheduling.description: Generate a balanced round-robin tournament schedule for N teams. Each team plays every other team exactly once (or twice in a double round-robin). The solver assigns games to time slots while balancing home and away games, avoiding back-to- back away stretches, and optionally minimizing total travel distance. The output is a complete fixture list with venues and dates.
tournament_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A regional basketball league has 6 teams. Each team plays every other team twice (home and away) over 10 matchdays. Teams should not have more than 2 consecutive away games. The league scheduler wants a fair, balanced fixture list.

track_maintenance_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule track maintenance windows to minimize service disruption while keeping infrastructure safe.
track_maintenance_scheduling.description: Plans when to perform maintenance on track sections over a planning horizon. Each section has a degradation state and a maintenance deadline. Maintenance requires a track possession (closure) window during which no trains can run. The model minimizes total service disruption (cancelled or rerouted trains) while ensuring every section is maintained before its safety deadline. Helps infrastructure managers coordinate maintenance with timetable planners.
track_maintenance_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A rail infrastructure company has four track sections requiring scheduled maintenance. Six overnight and weekend windows are available over the next three weeks. Each maintenance closure disrupts a different number of train services. The planner must schedule all maintenance before safety deadlines while minimizing total service disruptions.

train_timetabling.shortDescription: Create conflict-free train timetables that minimize total travel time and maximize line capacity.
train_timetabling.description: Assigns departure times and track segments to trains on a rail network so that no two trains occupy the same block at the same time. Each train has an origin, destination, desired departure window, and speed class. The model minimizes total delay (deviation from preferred schedule) while ensuring headway safety constraints between consecutive trains on shared tracks. Used by national rail operators and urban transit authorities for strategic and operational scheduling.
train_timetabling.scenarioDescription: Dutch Railways needs to schedule six trains (three express, two regional, one freight) on the Amsterdam-Rotterdam corridor over a 90-minute window. The two-segment corridor has a minimum headway of 5 minutes between trains. Express services have high priority and should depart close to their preferred times, while the freight train can be shifted if needed.

training_program_selection.shortDescription: Choose which training courses to offer employees to maximize skill coverage within a limited training budget.
training_program_selection.description: Select training programs from a catalog to maximize the number of skill gaps closed across the workforce within a fixed budget. Each program has a cost, a duration, and a set of skills it teaches. The solver picks the combination of programs that delivers the most skill improvement per euro spent while ensuring no employee exceeds their maximum training hours.
training_program_selection.scenarioDescription: An HR department has EUR 15,000 and 10 candidate training courses costing EUR 800 to EUR 3,000 each. The company needs to close gaps in 8 skill areas across 20 employees. Each course covers 1-4 skills. The HR manager wants the best-value training plan.

transportation_problem.shortDescription: Ship goods from supply points to demand points at minimum total shipping cost.
transportation_problem.description: The classic Hitchcock transportation problem. Given a set of supply nodes (factories, warehouses) and demand nodes (stores, customers), determine how many units to ship along each lane to minimize total transportation cost. Every supply node's output must be fully shipped and every demand node's requirement must be fully met. The output is a shipping plan showing flow on each lane.
transportation_problem.scenarioDescription: Three factories produce 300, 400, and 500 units respectively. Four retail stores need 250, 350, 200, and 400 units. Shipping cost per unit varies by factory-store pair from EUR 2 to EUR 9. The distribution manager wants the cheapest shipping plan.

transshipment.shortDescription: Route goods through intermediate hubs from origins to destinations at minimum cost.
transshipment.description: An extension of the transportation problem that includes intermediate transshipment nodes (hubs, cross-docks). Goods flow from supply nodes through zero or more intermediate nodes to demand nodes, and the solver minimizes total transportation cost. Each arc has a per-unit cost and capacity. The output shows optimal flows on every arc in the multi-echelon network.
transshipment.scenarioDescription: Two factories ship products to 4 retailers through 2 regional hubs. Direct factory- to-retailer shipping is expensive. Using hubs for consolidation is cheaper per unit but adds handling cost. The logistics planner wants the lowest-cost routing.

two_d_cutting.shortDescription: Cut rectangular pieces from standard sheets to minimize the number of sheets used.
two_d_cutting.description: Plan how to cut rectangular pieces from standard-size panels (plywood, glass, sheet metal) to fulfill orders while minimizing material waste. This simplified version uses a bin-packing approach where each piece's area is treated as its "size" and the sheet's area is the "bin capacity". For exact 2D layouts, a post-processing step would generate guillotine cuts.
two_d_cutting.scenarioDescription: A glass cutting workshop receives orders for 10 rectangular glass pieces of various sizes. Standard sheets are 2m x 3m (6 sqm each). The foreman wants to know the minimum number of sheets to cut.

vehicle_routing.shortDescription: Plan delivery routes for a fleet of vehicles to minimize total distance while meeting every customer's demand.
vehicle_routing.description: Solve the Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem (CVRP). A fleet of vehicles departs from a central depot, visits a set of customer locations with known demands, and returns to the depot. Each vehicle has a maximum load capacity. The solver finds routes that minimize total travel distance (or cost) while ensuring every customer is served exactly once and no vehicle is overloaded.
vehicle_routing.scenarioDescription: A food distributor operates 2 trucks (capacity 10 pallets each) from a central warehouse. Five restaurants need deliveries of 2-6 pallets today. The dispatcher needs routes that minimize total kilometres driven.

vessel_scheduling.shortDescription: Schedule cargo vessels across ports and time windows to maximize utilization and minimize voyage costs.
vessel_scheduling.description: Assigns cargo shipments to vessels and determines sailing schedules across a set of ports. Each vessel has a capacity, speed, and daily operating cost. Each shipment has an origin port, destination port, deadline, and revenue. The model maximizes total profit (revenue minus operating and fuel costs) while ensuring every accepted shipment is delivered on time and no vessel exceeds its cargo capacity. Used by tramp shipping companies and freight brokers.
vessel_scheduling.scenarioDescription: A tramp shipping company operates three cargo vessels positioned at Singapore, Rotterdam, and Dubai. Six cargo shipments need to be moved between these ports within a 30-day window. Each shipment has a deadline and revenue value. The company must decide which shipments to accept and assign to which vessel to maximize profit after operating costs.

warehouse_layout.shortDescription: Assign product categories to warehouse zones to minimize total picking travel distance.
warehouse_layout.description: Decide which product category goes into which warehouse zone to minimize the average distance pickers travel. High-frequency items should be near the dispatch area, heavy items near loading docks. The solver considers pick frequency, zone capacities, and inter-zone distances to produce an assignment that cuts daily picker travel. Use this before a warehouse reorganisation or when adding new product lines.
warehouse_layout.scenarioDescription: A distribution centre has 6 zones at varying distances from the dispatch dock. Eight product categories have different weekly pick frequencies. Zone capacities differ. The operations manager wants to re-slot categories to cut average pick travel by at least 20%.

warehouse_location.shortDescription: Choose which candidate warehouse sites to open to minimize total fixed plus shipping cost while covering all customers.
warehouse_location.description: Decide which warehouse locations to open from a set of candidates, and assign each customer to an open warehouse, minimizing the sum of facility opening costs and transportation costs. Each warehouse has a capacity and each customer has a demand. The solver selects the cost-optimal network design. The output tells you which sites to open and the customer-warehouse assignment.
warehouse_location.scenarioDescription: A logistics company evaluates 5 candidate warehouse sites to serve 8 customer regions. Opening costs range from EUR 50k to EUR 120k per year. Shipping costs depend on distance. Total demand is 10,000 pallets per year. The VP of operations wants the network with the lowest total annual cost.

warehouse_slotting.shortDescription: Assign SKUs to warehouse storage locations to minimize picking travel distance and maximize throughput.
warehouse_slotting.description: Assigns product SKUs to storage locations (slots) in a warehouse to minimize total daily picker travel distance. High-velocity items should be placed near packing stations; heavy items go on lower shelves. Each slot has a size class (pallet, case, each) and a proximity score to the dock. The model matches SKU characteristics (pick frequency, size, weight) to slot attributes while respecting slot capacity and product family adjacency preferences.
warehouse_slotting.scenarioDescription: An e-commerce fulfillment center has seven SKUs that need slot assignments across seven warehouse locations. High-pick-frequency items should be closest to the dock. Heavy palletized items must go on floor-level slots. The warehouse manager wants to minimize total daily picker walking distance.

waste_collection_routing.shortDescription: Plan garbage truck routes to collect waste from all sites at minimum total distance and fuel cost.
waste_collection_routing.description: Design collection routes for a fleet of waste trucks that must visit all pickup sites and return to the depot. Each truck has a weight capacity and each site has a known waste volume. The solver minimizes total distance (and therefore fuel cost and emissions) while ensuring every site is serviced. This is a specialized VRP variant for municipal waste management and recycling logistics.
waste_collection_routing.scenarioDescription: A city's waste department operates 3 trucks (capacity 8 tonnes each) from a central depot. Fifteen neighbourhood pickup points generate 0.5 to 2.0 tonnes per collection day. The route planner wants to minimise total kilometres driven while visiting every point.

wastewater_treatment_allocation.shortDescription: Route wastewater flows to treatment plants to minimize processing cost while meeting environmental discharge standards.
wastewater_treatment_allocation.description: Allocates wastewater from collection points to treatment facilities. Each facility has a capacity, treatment efficiency, and operating cost per unit. Different wastewater sources have varying contamination levels. The model minimizes total treatment and transport cost while ensuring all treated effluent meets regulatory discharge limits. Helps utilities plan capacity expansions and manage seasonal load variations.
wastewater_treatment_allocation.scenarioDescription: A city generates wastewater from four main sources with different contamination levels. Three treatment plants are available with varying capacities and treatment technologies. The utility must route all flows to plants such that treated effluent meets regulatory standards at minimum total cost.

water_distribution_network.shortDescription: Optimize water flow through a pipe network to meet demand at all nodes while minimizing pumping costs.
water_distribution_network.description: Routes water from treatment plants through a pipe network to consumption nodes (residential, commercial, industrial). Each pipe segment has a capacity and a pumping cost that depends on flow volume and elevation difference. The model minimizes total pumping energy cost while ensuring every demand node receives its required volume and no pipe exceeds its capacity. Helps utilities plan daily operations and identify bottlenecks.
water_distribution_network.scenarioDescription: A municipal water utility operates two treatment plants serving four consumption zones through a network of trunk mains. Pumping costs vary by pipe segment due to elevation and distance. The utility needs to route 850 cubic meters per hour to all zones at minimum pumping cost.

wildfire_resource_deployment.shortDescription: Deploy firefighting crews and equipment to active wildfires to minimize total area burned.
wildfire_resource_deployment.description: Allocates firefighting resources (crews, helicopters, water tankers) to active fire fronts across a region. Each fire has a growth rate, current size, and suppression difficulty. Each resource has an effectiveness rating, deployment cost, and travel time from its base. The model minimizes total expected area burned (or total damage cost) by deciding which resources to send where, subject to resource availability and deployment logistics.
wildfire_resource_deployment.scenarioDescription: A forestry agency is managing three active wildfires in a mountainous region. Five firefighting resources are available: two hotshot crews, an engine company, a helicopter, and an air tanker. Each fire has different growth rates and terrain difficulty. The incident commander must deploy resources to minimize the total area expected to burn over the next 24 hours.

workforce_assignment.shortDescription: Assign workers to tasks one-to-one to minimize total cost or maximize team efficiency.
workforce_assignment.description: Solve the classic assignment problem: given N workers and M tasks, assign each worker to exactly one task (or leave unassigned if there are more workers than tasks) such that total cost is minimized. Each worker-task pair has a known cost reflecting skill match, travel time, or hourly rate. The output is a complete assignment map with total cost.
workforce_assignment.scenarioDescription: A software agency must assign 3 developers (Alice, Bob, Charlie) to 3 project modules (Frontend, Backend, Testing). Each developer has different cost estimates per module based on skill fit. The project lead wants the cheapest staffing plan.
